title: Sarah King
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Sarah King
Snapshot: 2026-07-21

confidence.rationale: Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.

biography: I received my BA in Economics and Mathematics at Mercer University in Macon, GA, then my MA and PhD in Economics at Georgia State University in Atlanta, GA. My research focuses on education policy and am especially interested in equity issues in education and the experiences of teachers. I enjoy exploring new places (local and elsewhere), singing, watching sports, and spending time with my cats.
education_entries: Ph D in Economics, Georgia State University
MA in Economics, Georgia State University
BA in Economics and Mathematics, Mercer University
research_interests: Education policy, urban economics, experimental methods, and economics pedagogy
teaching_interests: Economics of Education, Urban Economics, Experimental Economics
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2025). Economic Lessons from Taylor Swift’s Song Lyrics . Journal of Economics Teaching.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2022). Learning-Mode Choice, Student Engagement, and Achievement Growth During the COVID-19 Pandemic . AERA Open. Volume, 8.
awards_honors: Tidewater Faculty Fellowship, CNU Center for Community Engagement. (2024)

Presentations: Sarah King, Jadrian Wooten, JET Symposium on Economics Teaching, "Taking Urban Economics to the Streets Through Faculty-Led Field Trips," West Lafayette, Indiana. (2026)
Sarah King, Adolph Brown, TeachECONference, "Teaching Economics through Community Engagement," Virtual. (2026)
Sarah King, Symposium on Economics Teaching, "Teaching Economics through Community-Engaged Learning," St. Louis, MO. (2025)
Sarah King, Symposium on Economics Teaching, "Economics is Bejeweled: Teaching Economics using Taylor Swift Songs," Blacksburg, VA. (2024)
Sarah King, Association for Education Finance and Policy Annual Meeting, "Does Summer School Work? Evidence from a 2021 Summer Program," Denver, CO. (2023)
